attacking_style: Pure sprinter who wins in final 200m through raw speed rather than positioning. Thrives when leadout trains disintegrate, capable of winning from compromised positions through superior acceleration.
team_tactics: Heavy reliance on tactical reconnaissance of technical finishes (UAE Tour, Paris-Nice). Teams shelter him all day but leadouts frequently fail or disintegrate inside final 3km, forcing him to rely on personal speed.
terrain_preference: Pancake-flat sprints and technical finishes with barriers and curves where recon provides advantage. Exposed and dropped on even moderate climbs with altitude, limiting Grand Tour stage hunting.
vulnerability: Chronic positioning issues and team support failures in flat sprints. Dropped immediately on any climb with altitude. Repeatedly caught out of position despite being marked as top sprinter (3+ observations).
key_patterns: Course reconnaissance critical to success: deliberately scouted UAE Tour and Paris-Nice finishes before wins.
Leadout trains consistently fail or disintegrate inside final 3km across multiple races and teams.
Positioning errors/failures documented on both flat sprints and before finale despite top sprinter status.
Cannot survive even moderate climbs at altitude; dropped and never rejoins in Grand Tours.
Raw speed compensates for poor positioning: wins from 15th-position disadvantage when others better placed.
form_trajectory: neutral: Won early 2023 (UAE Tour, Paris-Nice) but showed same positioning vulnerabilities. Speed intact but tactical/team issues persist chronically.
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 7
